title: Auto-refresh Issues tab after creating an issue
description: After a successful issue creation, automatically switch to the Issues tab and trigger a refresh so the new issue appears immediately without manual intervention.
design: Pass a callback or use a shared @State/ObservableObject to signal IssueListView to reload after CreateIssueView reports success.
acceptance_criteria: New issue appears in the Issues list immediately after creation without user needing to switch tabs or click Refresh.

title: Close / complete an issue from the UI
description: A Close button in the issue detail view (or swipe action on the row) that runs `bd close <id>` and removes the issue from the list.
design: Add Close button to IssueRow context menu and/or detail sheet. Run `bd close <id>` via BeadsRunner, then refresh the issues list on success.
acceptance_criteria: User can close an issue from the UI without touching the terminal. Issue disappears from the list after closing.

title: Issue detail view (bd show <id>)
description: Double-click a row in the Issues tab to open a sheet showing the full output of `bd show <id>`. Should display all fields: description, design notes, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and history.
design: Run bd show <id> via BeadsRunner, display output in a scrollable view or parse into labelled fields. Sheet presented as .sheet() on IssueListView.
acceptance_criteria: Double-clicking any issue row opens a detail sheet. All fields from bd show are visible. Sheet can be dismissed with Escape or a Close button.

title: GitHub Actions: signed macOS release workflow
description:
Create a `.github/workflows/release.yml` that builds, signs, notarizes, and publishes BeadsUI.app as a GitHub Release on version tags.

## Overview

The workflow is achievable because the project already builds via a plain `Makefile` + `swiftc` (no Xcode project needed). The main work is wiring up Apple code-signing and notarization inside the GitHub-hosted macOS runner.

## Feasibility assessment

**Straightforward parts**
- Build: `make` already works on any macOS runner with Xcode CLI tools installed
- Packaging: `hdiutil create` makes a DMG; `ditto -c -k --keepParent` makes a ZIP
- GitHub Release: `gh release create` or the `softprops/action-gh-release` action

**Parts that need care**
- Code signing: certificate + private key must be base64-encoded and stored as a repo secret, then imported into a temporary keychain on the runner
- Notarization: requires either (a) Apple ID + app-specific password + Team ID, or (b) App Store Connect API key (preferred — no 2FA friction)
- Stapling: `xcrun stapler staple` attaches the notarization ticket so the app works offline

## Required GitHub Secrets

| Secret | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `APPLE_CERTIFICATE_BASE64` | `base64 -i MyCert.p12` output |
| `APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD` | P12 export password |
| `APPLE_TEAM_ID` | 10-char Apple Developer Team ID |
| `APPLE_NOTARY_KEY_ID` | App Store Connect API key ID |
| `APPLE_NOTARY_KEY_ISSUER` | ASC issuer UUID |
| `APPLE_NOTARY_KEY_BASE64` | base64-encoded `.p8` private key |

(Alternatively: `APPLE_ID` + `APPLE_APP_PASSWORD` for legacy notarization)

## Workflow outline

```yaml
name: Release
on:
  push:
    tags: ['v*']
jobs:
  build-sign-release:
    runs-on: macos-14   # Apple Silicon runner
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Import signing certificate
        # create temp keychain, import .p12, set key partition list
      - name: Build
        run: make
      - name: Sign app bundle
        run: |
          codesign --deep --force --options runtime \
            --sign "Developer ID Application: NAME (TEAMID)" \
            BeadsUI.app
      - name: Package DMG
        run: hdiutil create -volname BeadsUI -srcfolder BeadsUI.app ...
      - name: Notarize
        run: |
          xcrun notarytool submit BeadsUI.dmg \
            --key-id $APPLE_NOTARY_KEY_ID \
            --issuer $APPLE_NOTARY_KEY_ISSUER \
            --key /tmp/auth.p8 \
            --wait
      - name: Staple
        run: xcrun stapler staple BeadsUI.dmg
      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          files: BeadsUI.dmg
```

## Entitlements consideration

Because BeadsUI shells out to `bd` (a CLI tool) and reads/writes the filesystem, a minimal entitlements plist is needed:
- `com.apple.security.app-sandbox`: false (or enable with file access entitlements)
- `com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit`: false
- Hardened Runtime must be enabled (`--options runtime` flag above)

If app-sandbox is disabled, notarization still succeeds but a warning is issued — this is fine for a developer tool.

## Acceptance criteria
- Pushing a `v*` tag triggers the workflow
- Resulting DMG is signed (`codesign -dv --verbose=4`) and notarized (`spctl -a -v`)
- DMG is attached to the GitHub Release automatically
- Secrets documented in CONTRIBUTING.md or a new RELEASE.md
- No hardcoded Team ID / identity strings in the workflow file

title: Resizable text areas in create/edit modals
description:
Add auto-resize and manual resize to all TextEditor fields in CreateIssueView and IssueEditSheet.

- Auto-grow: uses NSString.boundingRect to measure natural content height (capped at 220pt)
- Manual resize: AppKit NSView drag handle at bottom of each textarea; raw mouseDown/mouseDragged bypasses SwiftUI ScrollView gesture arbitration for accurate 1:1 drag tracking
- Resize cursor (NSCursor.resizeUpDown) on hover
- All call sites (description, design notes, acceptance criteria, notes, comment box) get behaviour automatically via formTextArea()

title: Match Create Issue sheet width to parent window
description: Capture NSApp.keyWindow width at button-tap time and pass to CreateIssueView so the sheet matches the parent window width, consistent with IssueEditSheet pattern.

title: Issues-primary layout: replace tabs with inline list + Create button
description: Remove tab navigation. Issues list becomes primary content view. Create button at top-LEFT (project-scoped action area). Create form opens as sheet with two submit modes: (1) Create Issue → returns to list, (2) Create & Add Another → resets form for batch entry.

title: Quit protection: confirm dialog and draft persistence for Create form
description: When quitting (Cmd+Q or red button) with a partially filled Create form, show a native macOS alert: Save Draft & Quit / Discard & Quit / Cancel. Draft is persisted to UserDefaults and offered for resumption on next launch.

title: Issue editor doesn't clear fields that are emptied on save
description: When editing an issue and clearing a field (e.g. Design Notes), the field was not saved as empty because BeadsRunner.update only passed flags for non-empty fields. Fixed by storing originalFields at load time and passing the flag (with empty value) for any field that was non-empty originally but is now empty.

title: Show child issues in edit sheet relationships section
description:
The edit sheet currently shows a Dependencies section (blocking/blocked-by/related relationships from `bd show --json` `dependencies` array). Child issues (issues with `parent` = this issue's ID) are not shown, even though they're a key relationship — especially relevant when editing epics.

Add a Children subsection to the relationships area in IssueEditSheet, showing the list of direct child issues.
design:
Data source:
- Investigate whether `bd show --json <id>` returns children inline (likely not, based on observed schema)
- Fallback: call `bd list --json` and filter for issues where `parent == id`; already parsed by buildTree()
- Or: `bd show --json` may support a flag to include children — check

If separate call needed:
- Add `BeadsRunner.listChildren(parentID: String, workingDirectory: String) throws -> [BeadsIssue]`
- Load alongside showDetail() in IssueEditSheet.task {}

Display:
- Add 'Children' subsection in the edit sheet, below or alongside Dependencies
- Each child row: status dot + id + title + priority badge (same style as dep rows)
- Children are read-only in this view (create child is a separate feature beads-ui-ef9)
- Consider showing 'No children.' when empty, or hiding the section entirely when empty
notes:
SCHEMA CONFIRMED: bd show --json already returns a `dependents` array alongside `dependencies`. Children appear in `dependents` with dependency_type: "parent-child". No separate CLI call needed.

Also: epic issues include epic_total_children, epic_closed_children, epic_closeable fields.

Implementation: add dependents: [BeadsDependency]? to BeadsIssueDetail; split edit sheet into Children subsection (parent-child dependents, read-only) + Dependencies subsection (existing interactive add/remove).

title: Show and add comments on issues (bd comments)
description:
Display existing comments on an issue and allow adding new ones from within the UI, without opening a terminal.

Related: beads-ui-ua6 covers `bd update --append-notes` (notes are a single append-only text field on the issue). Comments (`bd comments`) are separate threaded entries with author + timestamp and should be treated distinctly in the UI.
design:
Add a 'Notes & Comments' section below the form fields in IssueEditSheet (or as a secondary pane/tab within it).

Notes section:
- Load from `bd show --json` `notes` field (add to BeadsIssueDetail if not already present)
- TextEditor pre-populated with current notes
- 'Update Notes' button calls `bd update <id> --notes "..."` (replaces) or --append-notes (appends)
- Decision: replace vs append — probably offer both, or just append to be safe

Comments section:
- Load via `bd comments <id> --json` → array of {author, body, created_at} objects
- Display as a read-only list (author + timestamp + body)
- 'Add Comment' TextEditor + button → `bd comments add <id> "..."`
- Load comments alongside showDetail() in IssueEditSheet.task {}

BeadsRunner additions needed:
- `listComments(id:workingDirectory:) throws -> [BeadsComment]`
- `addComment(id:body:workingDirectory:) throws`

BeadsComment struct (Decodable):
- author: String
- body: String
- createdAt: Date (or String)
- (infer exact JSON keys from `bd comments --json` output when an issue has comments)
notes: Some test notes on this issue

comments.author: Charlie Garrison
comments.text: First test comment to check JSON schema

title: Open working directory in Terminal.app
description: A button in the top bar that opens the selected beads repository in Terminal.app (or the user's default terminal emulator).

title: Multi-repo support: pin and switch between repositories
description: Replace the single working-directory picker with a list of pinned repositories. The user can add/remove repos and switch between them from a sidebar or dropdown. Last-selected repo is remembered.

title: Load custom issue types from .beads/config.yaml
description: Read the selected repository's .beads/config.yaml to discover project-specific custom issue types and add them dynamically to the Type picker in the Create form.
design: Parse config.yaml for types.custom keys at repo selection time and when the Create tab is shown. Merge with built-in types. Fallback gracefully if config absent or unparseable.

title: Filter and search the Issues list
description: A search bar above the Issues list plus filter controls for status, priority, type, and label. Filters are passed as flags to `bd list --flat`.
design: Add a filter toolbar above the list with a TextField for title search and Pickers for status/priority/type. Rebuild the bd list args from active filters on change.

title: Add notes to an issue from the UI
description: A text field in the issue detail view to append notes to an existing issue without opening the terminal.
design: Add a notes text area + Append button to the detail sheet. Run `bd update <id> --append-notes "..."` via BeadsRunner.

title: Claim / start an issue (bd update --claim)
description: A Claim button in the detail view or row context menu that runs `bd update <id> --claim`, marking the issue as in-progress and assigning it to the current user.
design: Add Claim button to IssueRow and/or detail sheet. Run `bd update <id> --claim` via BeadsRunner, refresh list on success.

title: Add icon for the app
Create UI for `bd create-form`
description: Rather than being limited to CLI, I want a proper Mac app for creating issues for beads

title: Show real progress bar during issue load (two-phase)
description:
Currently the Issues tab shows a spinner with a live issue count while loading (streams stdout chunks). A better UX would be a determinate progress bar (0–100%).

Approach: two-phase load.
1. Fire `bd stats --json` first to get `open_issues` count (fast, < 1s).
2. Use that count as the `total` for a `ProgressView(value: loaded, total: total)` bar.
3. Existing `listWithProgress` stream provides the `value` as chunks arrive.

`bd stats --json` output shape:
```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total_issues": 142,
    "open_issues": 67
  }
}
```
Use `open_issues` (or `total_issues`) as the denominator.

Considerations:
- The stats call adds ~1 extra process launch; should be negligible vs the list call.
- If stats fails or count mismatches, fall back to indeterminate spinner gracefully.
- The count shown in the toolbar (`N issues`) should update live during load.

title: Per-project windows: open each repository in its own window
description: Allow the user to have multiple BeadsUI windows open simultaneously, each pointed at a different beads repository. A 'New Window' menu item (or Cmd+N) opens a fresh window with its own project picker.
design:
- WindowGroup already supports multiple instances; each gets independent @AppStorage or @State workingDirectory
- Add File > New Window command (keyboardShortcut .new)
- Each window manages its own recentProjects or shares a global list
- Window title shows project name

title: Open issue edit in a dedicated window
description:
When double-clicking an issue to edit, open IssueEditSheet in a standalone OS window instead of a modal sheet.

IssueEditSheet view is reused verbatim; only the presentation mechanism changes.
design:
1. Add Codable to BeadsIssue (required for openWindow(value:))
2. Add Window scene to BeadsUIApp.body: Window("Edit Issue", id: "edit-issue", for: BeadsIssue.self)
3. In IssueListView: @Environment(\.openWindow) var openWindow; replace .sheet presentation with openWindow(value: issue)
4. On save: post NotificationCenter notification; IssueListView subscribes and calls loadIssues()
5. Track set of open issue IDs to prevent duplicate windows for same issue

title: Split BeadsUI.swift into multiple files
description:
BeadsUI.swift is now ~900 lines. Split into 5 focused files for maintainability and more efficient AI-assisted editing (each targeted edit only loads the relevant file).

Proposed split:
- Models.swift — enums (IssueType, Priority, AppTab, BeadsError) + structs (BeadsIssue, BeadsIssueDetail, IssueFormFields)
- BeadsRunner.swift — all CLI interaction (create, list, show, showDetail, update, close, run helper)
- ContentView.swift — BeadsUIApp, ContentView, top bar, project picker
- CreateIssueView.swift — CreateIssueView, IssueFormContent, formField(), formTextArea()
- IssueListView.swift — IssueListView, IssueRow, IssueDetailSheet, IssueEditSheet

Makefile change: replace `BeadsUI.swift` with `*.swift` in the swiftc invocation (one line).

title: Multi-window and multi-project UI
description:
Umbrella epic for making BeadsUI a proper multi-window, multi-project macOS citizen.

Three related but separable capabilities:
1. Issue edit in its own window (original scope)
2. Per-project windows — one window per beads repository
3. Tabbed interface — projects as native macOS tabs in a single window, with drag-out to separate windows

The tabbed approach (3) is the most ambitious and essentially subsumes (2). macOS native window tabbing (NSWindowTabbing) gives drag-out-to-new-window for free once tabs are implemented.
design:
Issue edit window:
- BeadsIssue: Codable conformance
- Window scene: Window("Edit Issue", id: "edit-issue", for: BeadsIssue.self)
- openWindow(value: issue) replaces .sheet call
- NotificationCenter post on save → IssueListView reloads
- Guard: track open issue IDs to avoid duplicate windows

Per-project windows:
- WindowGroup with per-project identity
- Each window gets its own workingDirectory state
- New Window menu item opens a fresh project picker

Tabbed interface:
- NSWindowTabbing or SwiftUI TabView at window level
- Each tab = one project (its own IssueListView + workingDirectory)
- Native macOS tab bar: tabs detach to new windows by drag
- Project picker per tab, not global
- Shared recentProjects list across tabs/windows

title: bd create --dry-run preview before submitting
description: A Preview button in the Create form that runs `bd create --dry-run` with the current field values and shows what would be created, before the user commits.

title: Right-click context menu on issue rows
description: Inline actions on issue rows via right-click: Copy ID, Copy title, Mark in progress, Mark closed, Open detail. Avoids needing to open a full detail sheet for quick actions.

title: Sort and group the Issues list
description: Allow the Issues list to be sorted by priority, status, created date, or updated date, and optionally grouped by type or label.

title: Auto-refresh Issues list with background polling
description: An option to poll `bd list` every N seconds and update the Issues tab in the background, similar to `bd list --watch`. Configurable interval, off by default.

title: System notification on successful issue creation
description: Post a macOS UserNotification when an issue is created, showing the issue ID and title, with a Copy ID action button.

title: Menu bar extra for quick issue creation
description: A lightweight menu bar icon that lets the user file a quick issue (title only, using defaults for type/priority) without opening the main window.

title: Preferences panel: configurable bd path and defaults
description: A Preferences panel (⌘,) where the user can configure: path to the bd binary (for non-standard installs), default issue type, default priority, and default assignee.

title: Keyboard shortcuts: tab switching and refresh
description: Add keyboard shortcuts: ⌘1/⌘2 to switch between Create and Issues tabs, ⌘R to refresh the Issues list, ⎋ to dismiss banners and sheets.

title: Restore windows to correct Space on relaunch
description:
When BeadsUI is quit with windows on different macOS Spaces and relaunched, windows are not restored to their original Spaces (they all reappear on the current Space).

Window positions/sizes and per-window project (@SceneStorage) ARE correctly restored — only Space assignment is not.

## Investigation notes (2026-04-17)

- `NSQuitAlwaysKeepsWindows = true` added to Info.plist — did not fix Space restoration
- Space restoration is controlled by the macOS window server, not the app directly
- There is no public AppKit/SwiftUI API to assign a window to a specific Space at launch time
- `NSWindow.collectionBehavior` can control whether a window appears on all Spaces (.canJoinAllSpaces, .moveToActiveSpace) but not pin to a specific Space
- The private API `CGSMoveWindowsToManagedSpace` exists but is unsupported and could break any macOS version

## Possible approaches to investigate

1. **CGSPrivate / private SkyLight APIs** — fragile, unsupported, not recommended
2. **Mission Control / Spaces index** — no public API to query or set
3. **Accept as macOS limitation** — many native apps (including some Apple apps) do not restore Spaces; this may be the correct resolution

## Recommendation

Likely accept as a macOS platform limitation unless a clean supported API is found. Close as won't-fix if no solution emerges.

title: Tabbed interface: projects as native macOS tabs
description:
Projects live as native macOS tabs within a single window. Tabs can be dragged out to become separate windows (standard macOS tab behaviour). Each tab is an independent project view with its own workingDirectory.

This is the most ambitious option and essentially supersedes the per-project-windows feature.
design:
Two approaches:
A) NSWindowTabbing (AppKit): enable preferred tab style on the main window; macOS adds tab bar automatically; each WindowGroup instance becomes a tab. Low SwiftUI control.
B) Custom TabView at root level with per-tab ContentView instances, each holding independent state. More control but custom tab chrome.

Approach A is closer to native macOS conventions and likely less code. The tab bar appearance matches system apps (Terminal, Safari).

title: GitHub sync: clickable external-ref URLs in detail view
description: Display --external-ref values (e.g. gh-123, jira-ABC-456) as clickable hyperlinks in the issue detail view, opening the linked issue in the default browser.

title: Shortcuts app integration: expose Create Issue as an action
description: Expose a Create Issue action to the macOS Shortcuts app, allowing users to automate issue creation from other apps, scripts, or Siri.

title: Spotlight integration: index open issues
description: Index open beads issues so they appear in macOS Spotlight search results, allowing users to jump to an issue or copy its ID directly from Spotlight.

title: Dependency visualisation (blocking/blocked-by graph)
description: A simple graph view of issue dependencies showing blocking and blocked-by relationships, accessible from the detail sheet.

title: Create child issues from the detail view
description: When viewing an issue's detail sheet, expose a Create Child button that opens the Create form pre-populated with --parent <id>, allowing hierarchical issue creation without touching the terminal.
